refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide teleoperation control commands and telemetry with guaranteed delivery within packet loss limits.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time network quality monitoring and adaptive retransmission protocol.
DP2: Use of redundant communication channels to mitigate packet loss.
DP3: Compression and prioritization of teleoperation data to optimize bandwidth usage.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Real-time network quality monitoring and adaptive retransmission protocol.

potential_sources: Interaction between network monitoring and teleoperation command processing.
Dependency on network link interface quality.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmission impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency monitoring as a complementary FR to address retransmission delay impact.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware modules responsible for teleoperation communication, including network monitoring and packet management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the communication network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time status and quality metrics of teleoperation communication to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions from higher-level control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but low containment of teleoperation-specific packet loss management.
Primarily physical control, minimal containment of communication quality logic.
High containment and low diffusion; best fit for packet loss management.
Focus on safety alerts, not communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning packet loss management to Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality functions, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if packet loss impacts safety alerts.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate interface definitions with Safety Services to handle safety-critical communication failures.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive retransmission protocol) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining packet loss within limits with clear verification methods.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as packet loss management is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting clear ownership and modularity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to DP and IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add latency monitoring FR to complement packet loss management.
Define coordination protocols with Safety Services for safety-critical communication events.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, adding latency monitoring and clarifying safety coordination will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide teleoperation control commands and telemetry with guaranteed delivery within packet loss limits.

revise.why: To include latency monitoring and safety coordination as explicit functional requirements.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement adaptive retransmission without excessive latency.
open_risks: Latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness due to retransmissions.
Interface coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
evidence_needed: Performance data on retransmission latency under expected network conditions.
Interface specifications and protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
